schema1.description: Describes the survey and declares the columns ('fields') that each response below will provide values for

schema2.description: Display name of the survey shown in TheyDo

schema3.description: Stable unique identifier for this survey. Re-uploading a file with the same surveyId updates the existing data source instead of creating a new one

schema4.description: Declares every column that appears in this survey's responses, and how each one should be interpreted on import

schema5.description: Stable identifier for this field, referenced by responses[].responseFields[].fieldId

schema6.description: Display label for this field/column shown in TheyDo

schema7.description: How this field's values should be imported. TEXT (default): imported as plain text with no special handling. TAG_GROUP: each response's value for this field is coded as a tag inside the tag group named by tagGroupTitle (a tag group is a named category of tags used to classify feedback, e.g. 'Sentiment' containing tags like Positive/Neutral/Negative). PERSONA: each response's value identifies which persona (an existing customer/user segment in the TheyDo workspace) that response belongs to — at most one field per file may use PERSONA. IGNORE: the column exists in the source data but is skipped on import.

schema8.description: Required when fieldType is TAG_GROUP: the display name of the tag group this field's values should be coded into. Matched case-insensitively against tag groups that already exist in the workspace; if no match is found, a new tag group is created with this exact title — so a typo creates a new, unintended tag group rather than raising an error

schema9.description: Whether this field must have a non-empty value in every response

schema10.description: Controls how TheyDo's AI turns responses into quotes (the atomic unit of customer feedback in TheyDo). Default false: AI analyzes each response's text and extracts zero, one, or multiple quotes from it, as it judges appropriate — best for long free-text or transcript-style answers. Set to true to skip that extraction step and import every response row as exactly one quote, verbatim — best when each response is already a short, atomic answer (e.g. a single survey question) that doesn't need AI interpretation

schema11.description: The actual survey responses. Each response provides one value per declared field via responseFields

responseDateTime.description: When this response was submitted, as an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp ending in 'Z' (e.g. 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z). Naive datetimes or numeric offsets (e.g. +02:00) are rejected — convert to UTC first

responseFields.description: The values this response provided, one entry per field it answers

fieldId.description: Must match a fieldId declared in surveyMetadata.surveyFields, and must be unique within this response

textValue.description: The raw text value provided for this field in this response
